summary.business_type: SaaS - indie financial planning / retirement simulator (freemium)
top_findings: Canonical, hreflang, og:url, and sitemap all built from a non-www site config that 308-redirects to www in production
No CSP / X-Content-Type-Options / Referrer-Policy / Permissions-Policy headers
SoftwareApplication schema asserts price: 0 despite a paid Premium tier
No last-reviewed date or author credential on the tax/methodology page
FAQPage schema has no SERP benefit post May-2026 FAQ rich result retirement (keep for AI citability only)
quick_wins: public/robots.txt: point Sitemap: line at the www host
vercel.json: add a headers block with baseline security headers
MarketingLayout.astro: fix or remove the Offer schema price assertion

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: robots.txt correctly disallows /app/, /*/app/, /api/ while allowing marketing pages
HSTS enabled, single-hop clean redirect from non-www to www
Valid sitemap index + sitemap generated via @astrojs/sitemap
No noindex leaks on marketing pages; robots meta correct on all sampled pages

findings.title: Canonical/hreflang/sitemap point to a redirecting host
findings.description: astro.config.mjs sets site: 'https://runaway.money' (no www). Production Vercel domain redirects non-www to www.runaway.money (308, confirmed live). Every canonical tag, hreflang alternate, og:url, and sitemap URL is generated from Astro.site and therefore points at a URL that immediately redirects, verified across all 8 crawled pages.
findings.recommendation: Change astro.config.mjs `site` to https://www.runaway.money and update the Sitemap: line in public/robots.txt to match.

findings.title: Missing security response headers
findings.description: Only strict-transport-security is present in live response headers. No Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy. vercel.json's headers block only defines a cache-control rule for static assets.
findings.recommendation: Add a headers block to vercel.json with baseline CSP, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy, scoped so it doesn't break the /app SPA.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Methodology and About pages are well-structured (~540-590 words) with clear H2/H3 hierarchy
Methodology page discloses model limitations ("Ce que le modele ne peut pas savoir") - good trust signal
Clear non-advice disclaimer present in FAQ and About copy

findings.title: No freshness or authorship signal on tax/methodology content
findings.description: /methodologie and /en/methodology explain tax and inflation modeling assumptions but show no visible last-reviewed date or author credential, a real E-E-A-T gap for YMYL-adjacent financial content where stale assumptions are a credibility risk.
findings.recommendation: Add a visible "last reviewed" date and a short author credential note to the methodology pages.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Unique, descriptive titles and meta descriptions on every page in both fr and en
Unique, outcome-focused H1s (not just brand name)
Clean heading hierarchy, no skipped levels observed
hreflang structure (fr/en/x-default) correctly implemented, only needs host corrected

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Organization, SoftwareApplication, FAQPage, and WebPage schema all present and well-formed JSON-LD

findings.title: SoftwareApplication Offer misstates pricing
findings.description: MarketingLayout.astro hardcodes offers: { price: '0', priceCurrency: 'USD' } even though the product has a paid Premium tier (Monte Carlo simulation per the homepage FAQ).
findings.recommendation: Drop the offers field or model it as an AggregateOffer reflecting both the free and Premium tiers.

findings.title: FAQPage schema has no SERP rich-result benefit anymore
findings.description: Google retired FAQ rich results for all sites on May 7, 2026. The existing FAQPage markup will not produce a SERP feature, but remains useful as an AI/LLM citation signal.
findings.recommendation: Keep the existing FAQPage markup for AI citability; don't add further FAQPage blocks expecting a Google SERP rich result.

categories.name: Performance
what_works: Homepage JS entry chunk is a 0-byte hydration stub; the interactive app is excluded from marketing pages and correctly gated behind robots.txt

findings.title: No Core Web Vitals lab or field data available this run
findings.description: Keyless PageSpeed Insights hit a shared-IP rate limit (240 QPM / 25,000 QPD) and no Search Console/CrUX/GA4 credentials are configured, so this category could not be scored numerically.
findings.recommendation: Configure a PageSpeed Insights API key or Search Console access, then re-run this category.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: FAQ-format content and disclosed methodology are strong raw material for AI Overview / LLM citation once the canonical signal is fixed

findings.title: No llms.txt
findings.description: No llms.txt found at the site root (404).
findings.recommendation: Add an llms.txt describing the product, methodology page, and non-advice disclaimer.

categories.name: Images
what_works: Hero product screenshot has descriptive alt text with responsive srcset
Decorative icons correctly use empty alt=""

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Update Sitemap: line in public/robots.txt to the www host

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add security headers block to vercel.json
Fix or remove the SoftwareApplication Offer price assertion

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Add last-reviewed date and author credential to methodology pages
Plan a content surface beyond the 8 core pages

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Configure Search Console + PageSpeed API key for field data
Re-run Common Crawl backlink check periodically
Capture a drift baseline against www.runaway.money
